title: Gendos Control Shinji Blind to Bardiel Killing
display_title: Gendos Control Shinji Blind to Bardiel Killing
wikitext:
[]: Gendo's Control - Shinji Blind to Bardiel Killing             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Apr 10, 2011 8:03 pm I was just watching the Bardiel scene, and I noticed an interesting detail about it that contrasts severely with the original. When the Dummy System activates, Shinji loses sight of the scene and can only hear a bit of what's going on. In the original, however, Shinji had a front row seat to witness the brutal destruction of Unit-03 (and its pilot), committed by his own hands. This is an interesting change in detail. There's certainly a great deal of difference in the impact that is caused by watching the murder with your own eyes in comparison to not being able to see or know what's going on until it's already been done. What possible meaning could there be behind this change? Is Anno trying to tell us something about what it means to and not to witness killing done using their hands without having a choice? To hell if I knew. And before we begin this discussion, I'm gonna save us some time and dispute the inevitable naysayers that will argue that Shinji could see what was going on while the Dummy System was active: Here we see as the Dummy System activates, the interior of the plug is lit up with this bright red background. The outside cannot be seen from within the plug as it usually is under normal circumstances. This essentially makes Shinji retreat into his mind as his body is used against his will. Here we see Shinji trying to find a way to see what's going on, but finds he cannot see anything at all, other than the bright red lights. From his mental prison he cannot see anything beyond itself, and he's blind to what he's being used for. It turns out that Shinji can still communicate, or at least still be heard from beyond his shell. But nobody is communicating back to him or telling him anything about the situation, further emphasizing his blindness to what is happening around him. Lastly, Shinji notices the sound of Unit-01 clenching the Entry Plug in its teeth. All he perceives is this sound, yet he immediately realizes what it is, or at least manages to hit the jackpot when he fears for the worst: Unit-01 is going to destroy the Entry Plug. So, just what is the significance of this difference? Discuss!

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Apr 10, 2011 8:28 pm The fact that he doesn't have to witness it is more humane. It probably reflects on Gendo in Rebuild, and supposedly how.. "generous" or "kind" he is in comparison to the one in NGETV. In Rebuild, acts as if he is acting back on the protection of his son, rather then his own desires (this could be observed on other occasions as well).

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Apr 10, 2011 9:07 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Sun Apr 10, 2011 9:34 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: I thought, and I'm sure this is going to shock all of you, that this particular change was pointless and stupid. If his senses are so cutoff by the Dummy System, how would he know the exact moment that Eva-01 has the plug in its mouth? Just by touch? Why is touch still active if sight isn't? He's still more effected by the event than the NGE incarnation, and understandably so due to knowing who it was beforehand and said person being his cute roommate, so I just don't get the point of this particular change. It's needless, lame, and confusing in a bad way.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Apr 10, 2011 9:43 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Sun Apr 10, 2011 9:55 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Well, that's even more preposterous. How would hearing anything really indicated it was the Entry Plug being crushed? It turns the simultaneous climactic scream of Shinji and Bardiel/Asuka into a typical emotive movie contrivance instead of something presented realistically in the original. Yet another aspect kept because of lazy writing, combined with aspects changed because of clueless writing. re: more 'humane': The whole point of the scene remains to brutally shock Shinji into breaking completely with Eva and his father until he's drawn back for the right reasons... the entire point is for Shinji to be utterly appalled and shocked. Trying to also make it 'humane' completely undercuts this. Add on top the hideous lack of sympathy or care on the writers part regarding the victim seen on display both in the scene and afterwards in both the series and the movies (so far) it's rather disgusting if there was an attempt to make things more humane for Shinji while continuing to whitewash the fate of the victim. Basically I'm not happy with any of this.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:05 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:13 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: Would Gendo have had any control over whether the Dummy System cuts off visuals? In the movies the Dummy System was made at an entirely different Nerv base. As to whether it's 'more humane', I simply disagree, and continue to be disgusted at how much LESS humane Asuka's treatment was, both in and out of universe.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:20 pm For whatever it's worth, some stuff from the 2.22 booklet in hopes of adding to this discussion: Eva 2.22 BD/DVD booklet wrote: Eva Unit-03 and Dummy System The color design for Eva Unit-03 (The Ninth Angel) was changed, and a 3D model created, with the basic animation in contrast to Test Type-01. The Dummy System was a new design proposed by Ikuto Yamashita. This design arose from a request for the action to proceed as if Shinji had his body and vision blocked and could only hear sound. Through gooey, slow movements achieved through CG, we obtained a scene that could ooze physiological revulsion.

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:26 pm Sachi wrote: Gendo doesn't literally have control over the Dummy Plug's actions, but it can be applied symbolically. Gendo wished to control Shinji to get the job done, so he sent the Dummy System, which is symbolic of that control Gendo wanted. LordEvan88's analogy makes sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireand'chutes77 » Mon Apr 11, 2011 12:36 am I was going to make a post to the contrary, but thankfully I watched the relevant section of the original series first. I remembered the plug going red; I didn't recall that Shinji's viewscreens still remained see-through. Now that that's out of the way, I agree that the restraints are an effective touch. I thought there was a slight incongruity between Shinji's horror at killing "with his own hands" and the reality of this scene in the original series, as it seemed he was free to remove his hands from the controls and see that they were acting without him. When his hands are locked to the controls, though, even if he can't control them... the horror cranks up to 11. Since the control arms are hard-wired into the rest of the control system, they would physically translate the vibrations of what was happening outside more clearly than the LCL matrix, even if the neural connections were disabled. On a side note: The sound of that turbine spooling up is still terrifying...

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Apr 11, 2011 7:40 am One can assume that he DID see the results after the Dummy Plug stopped working. As he heard stuff and was forced to move his hands and so on, he might've ended up with the taste of Asuka's blood in his mouth . *schudders* I think the lack of visuals is nothing deliberate that's meant to be "humane", but simply the way the Rebuild!Dummyplug works. The NGE one didn't require the hand restraints, either. Note how Asuka gets to see similar light phenomenons. Interpretation-wise, as the darkness isn't really "shielding" Shinji from anything (he still hears/feels it/knows that something very bad is going on), so I'd say it's probably just meant to emphasize his helplessness.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Apr 11, 2011 8:16 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: Well, that's even more preposterous. How would hearing anything really indicated it was the Entry Plug being crushed?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Apr 11, 2011 12:17 pm I agree. It adds much more to the sense of helplessness and the amount of control Gendo imposes upon Shinji. Gendo even insisted that the Dummy Plug be installed before Unit-01 was sent out. Does this mean he was aware that Shinji would refuse to fight? In all likelihood, no. However, his order does serve two purposes. 1) The first is fairly straightforward: his order is foreshadowing the use of the Dummy System. 2) It further symbolizes the control Gendo has in this situation. First, with usual Operations Director (Misato) taken out of the picture, Gendo is left in charge of the Evangelion operations. He then orders the Dummy Plug be installed in the Unit-01, symbolizing his control as Operations Director and focusing his influence on Unit-01. Lastly, when Shinji refuses to fight, Gendo takes control of the situation by initiating the Dummy System. Symbolically, Gendo is removing Shinji's free will and forcing him to act against his wishes. The restraints emphasize the "using my own hands" part, while the blindness adds to the claustrophobia and inability to fully tell what's going on. This all demonstrates Shinji's utter lack of control in this situation, and the sense of rape and violation on his part. On a broader scale, the Dummy System is also symbolic of Gendo's control of Yui. As we see when Zeruel shows up, however, Unit-01 refuses the Dummy System, and Gendo interprets it as Yui refusing him. Kendrix wrote: Note how Asuka gets to see similar light phenomenons.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 2:12 pm I'm sure most people realize this, but, since the dummy system (an artificial pilot) is put in control of the Eva, and Shinji is shackled and blinded, the "Shinji kills Eva-03/hurts Asuka" thing is loosely symbolic and only really true on a psychological level. As "reality", it's about as illogical as, say: some renegade computer program taking over a guy's fighter jet and taking down the jet of the pilot's friend, and then the pilot blames himself, even though he had nothing to do with it. This is just a pet peeve of mine. IT'S AN IMPORTANT DISTINCTION DAMMIT. Sachi wrote: Gendo even insisted that the Dummy Plug be installed before Unit-01 was sent out.

[]:             Quote Post by Jessemon » Mon Apr 11, 2011 2:28 pm Eh, I thought the new dummy system was just an attempt by Anno to keep the same tension/emotion from the same scene in NGE while simultaneously pulling his old "making stuff in Rebuild that was in NGE different for no (yet) discernible reason." The first time, Shinji could see everything that was going on, but he wasn't really participating in any way (besides by being in the cockpit). In Rebuild, he loses the primary way in which he experienced the situation in NGE (by sight), but is also forced to "participate" in the destruction by having his hands stuck to the control sticks by the new-fangled Dummy System.

[]:             Quote Post by Volume11 » Mon Apr 11, 2011 2:41 pm @Reichu- I would argue that the dummy plug in 2.22 using physical restraints that hold Shinji's hands in place for him to feel the movements and reactions as if he IS killing Asuka against his will, versus simply being an ineffectual bystander, to be more than a psychological trauma...or perhaps it creates an even stronger emotion that it is a physical act he joined in..... Which is also why I think it was an effective choice. He was robbed of sight and free will yet still went through the phsical routine of killing Bardiel/Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 2:52 pm Volume11 wrote: yet still went through the phsical routine of killing Bardiel/Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by LordEvan88 » Mon Apr 11, 2011 3:15 pm Reichu wrote: (I wonder if the designers of the dummy system core control module considered that the whole "pinning you to your seat" thing totally wouldn't work the second time around.)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 11, 2011 4:24 pm LordEvan88 wrote: So perhaps the contraption that secures the controls is necessary to transfer sync from the pilot to the system
